key_insight: The paper replaces nonconvex PINN training with a convex, stepwise least-squares solve by combining quasilinearization with linear-in-parameters physics-informed trial spaces.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more stable and often parameter-efficient route for solving nonlinear PDEs in engineering simulations, especially when reliable convergence matters more than end-to-end gradient training.
method: Use Bellman-Kalaba quasilinearization to turn a nonlinear PDE into a sequence of linear subproblems.
Discretize each subproblem with collocation on a LiL trial space, such as random features, spectral polynomials, or trigonometric bases.
Solve each step directly with QR-based linear least squares instead of gradient descent.
evidence_signal: The method was tested on seven benchmarks, including Bratu, Burgers, Buckley-Leverett, elasticity, Navier-Stokes in 2D/3D, and Darcy flow, with single-digit outer iterations in most cases and machine-precision recovery when the exact solution lies in the trial space.
watch_item: Convergence is local and depends on a smallness condition, so performance may degrade if the initial guess is poor or the solution lies outside the trial space.
paper_title: A Convex Quasilinearization Method for Solving Nonlinear PDEs with Physics-Informed Neural Networks
